register.note: No hero: hero.png opens on a title + 4-line lede + metadata card with the entire left-center canvas empty cream, where ciechanowski-watch/00-viewport.png lands a rendered 3D watch with a drag/slider invitation as the page's face (forced 0).
No hero exists to depict the bias-variance U or residuals the way seeing-theory-regression/01 paints squared-error boxes onto the OLS line, so there is no protagonist to judge (forced 0).

The see-spine graphic self-labels (title, axis ticks, train/test scorecards, 'sweet spot' marker, legend) but unlike distill-momentum/02 — which names each behavior in-figure ('Ripples','Divergence') with inline math — our filled-vs-hollow train/held-out distinction and the residual mechanism are explained only in adjacent prose, not on the canvas.

Cream ground, soft curves and restraint read calm, but focal hierarchy is weak — see-viewport shows ~180px+ of dead cream between the header card and the four-pass nav with no hero for the eye to land on, where ciechanowski-watch/02 fuses graphic + coloured prose + slider into one composed instrument.

The degree slider drives the fit curve and the sweet-spot marker, which is explanatory not decorative, but from static frames I see no proof of stepped/choreographed reveal of the bias-variance decomposition comparable to 3b1b-gradient-descent/02's frame-by-frame cost build-up, so it cannot be rewarded above competent.

Semantic grammar holds and is carried into prose (amber=data, blue=fit, grey=train, crimson=test/honest with 'wriggle' tinted to match the test-error line, purple=chosen degree) with no all-red field draining 'error', matching r2d3's shared-vocabulary discipline; only nit is the inline crimson term at ~4.14:1 (under body-AA, mitigated by underline+weight).

hero.note: A hero would need a full-width composed bias-variance U or a wiggling-fit-vs-residuals plot as the page's face, self-labeled and legible as a thumbnail, replacing the empty cream beside the title.

assessment.note: Item 5 is a genuine novel-scenario transfer (teammate scores 0.99 train / 0.62 held-out) but is mechanically a 3-option MCQ, not an interaction with the live U widget or an open prompt.

blocking: Compose a full-width hero specimen as the page's face (e.g. the bias-variance U or the wiggling fit with held-out residuals) so hero-as-protagonist and mechanism-in-the-picture stop being forced 0.
Annotate the see graphic in-canvas: label filled=train vs hollow=held-out and call out the residual/turn on the curve rather than deferring meaning to prose.
Kill the ~180px of dead cream around the see-viewport header and give the eye a focal anchor so atmosphere reads as a composed instrument, not a blog with a figure.
Lift the crimson inline term ('wriggle') to >=4.5:1 on cream to clear body-text AA, keeping underline+weight as the secondary cue.
Make the transfer item interactive or open (have learners drive the live U widget to the failure, or answer in their own words) instead of a third MCQ.
verdict.summary: Disciplined colour and competent annotation/atmosphere/motion, but no composed hero and three judgeable dims at 2 leave it below the benchmark bar — hold.
